messages.content:
Let me start by loading the execution-trace skill as required, then the CSV mapper skill.







Now I'll parse and map all 13 CSV rows to PackagePlan JSON. Let me apply the skill rules directly.



Applying the full mapper logic now to produce all 13 PackagePlan records, then saving them all simultaneously to Cosmos DB:





---

## ✅ Import Complete — `Packages_With_Services.csv`

All records were parsed, mapped, and saved automatically.

| Metric | Count |
|---|---|
| 📄 Total rows in CSV | 13 |
| ✅ Successfully saved to Cosmos DB | **13** |
| ❌ Failed | **0** |

---

### 📦 Saved Records

| # | Package Code | Package Name | Price | Benefits | Cosmos DB ID |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | PKG2 | 10 lesson Pack | $750.00 | 1 service | `4b90cd39-0d1e-4918-8e99-ef2bbc2abddb` |
| 2 | 10pack0002 | 10 Pack (silver pack) | $675.00 | 1 service | `52fafdc8-71bd-47c0-a7f7-25dc45daba27` |
| 3 | 10pracpack | 10 practice Pack | $400.00 | — | `7328cedd-be49-467e-b255-bb72d10bd6cd` |
| 4 | PKG3 | 20 lesson Pack | $1,400.00 | — | `770db644-1726-4b24-9aaf-970c54836edf` |
| 5 | PKG4 | 30 lesson Pack | $2,000.00 | — | `ac66e9e6-f062-4e8c-8cb7-07373b990be4` |
| 6 | PKG1 | 5 lesson Pack | $400.00 | — | `71d98db4-39ef-4b98-a7b7-954589c3b5fd` |
| 7 | PKG7 | 50 lesson Pack | $3,000.00 | — | `0bdff7b6-128e-42ae-b302-0d56f2ef7a71` |
| 8 | 5010pk | 50% off 10 Pack | $0.00 | — | `29671013-8dc2-4aa0-8877-f3178d661def` |
| 9 | corpevnt | Corporate Event Package | $400.00 | — | `3ac85967-679f-4eaa-b910-36414ea7c559` |
| 10 | imprvbundl | Golf Improvement Bundle | $599.00 | — | `1d492c20-894d-46b8-95cc-69ac33c061a0` |
| 11 | promo_PBR1 | Promotion Bay Rental | $0.00 | — | `af8bdaa3-4404-40a7-a55c-5e5bce9e6d35` |
| 12 | promo_PSE | Promotion Swing Evaluation | $0.00 | — | `9201e28e-bb5a-46f9-8a38-e4d132bb27de` |
| 13 | 3PACKAGE | Three Pack | $240.00 | — | `63b5749d-71f4-4652-af4b-1f798d1bf8e5` |

---

### 🗺️ Column Mapping Summary

| CSV Column | → Target Field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `Id` | `packageId` | Original CSV ID preserved here |
| `Name` | `packageName` | Direct match |
| `Code` | `packageCode` | Direct match |
| `Desc` | `description` | Fuzzy match (desc → description) |
| `Price` | `price` | Direct match, cast to float |
| `CategoryName` | `packageCategory` | Semantic match |
| `BusinessUnitId` | `businessId` | Semantic match (businessUnit id) |
| `BusinessUnit` | `businessName` | Semantic match |
| `Active` | `isActive` | Boolean parsed (TRUE/FALSE) |
| `OnlineBooking` | `onlineBookingEnabled` | Semantic match |
| `PackageOwnerType` | `ownerType` | Direct match |
| `PackageOwnerName` | `ownerName` | Direct match |
| `Services` (JSON) | `benefits[]` | Parsed embedded JSON array |
| *(auto-generated)* | `id` | New GUID assigned by Cosmos DB |
| *(constant)* | `priceSource` | Always `"FromSourceFile"` |
| *(constant)* | `taxGroup`, `centerTaxId` | Always `null` |

**⚠️ Skipped / Unmapped CSV columns** (no matching target field):

| CSV Column | Reason |
|---|---|
| `VersionId` | No target field in PackagePlan schema — skipped |
| `Type` | Ambiguous (numeric `25` for all rows); no matching target — skipped |
| `TaxGroupName` | `taxGroup` is always `null` per schema rules — skipped |
| `CenterTaxId` | `centerTaxId` is always `null` per schema rules — skipped |
| `CategoryId` | No target field; `packageCategory` uses the name not the ID — skipped |

---

> 💡 **Note on benefits:** Only rows 1 & 2 had populated `Services` JSON — their `benefits` arrays contain `Single Lesson 30 min` with 10 credits. All other rows had empty or null `Services` and received `benefits: []`.

My plan: 1) Load the execution-trace skill first, 2) Load the csv-packageplan-mapper skill, 3) Run the mapper script/process to parse and map all CSV rows to PackagePlan JSON, 4) Auto-save every record to Cosmos DB via cosmos_create_document, 5) Report results back to user."}

{"event":"thought","description":"I must load the execution-trace skill first before anything else, as per instructions. I'll also simultaneously load the csv-packageplan-mapper skill since both are needed."}

{"type":"reflection","step":1,"plugin":"execution-trace","plugin_type":"skill","result_summary":"Execution trace skill loaded — instructs emitting structured JSON trace events throughout execution.","insight":"Trace pattern confirmed. I'll emit plan/thought/reflection/summary events as structured JSON in <trace> tags throughout.","status":"success"}

{"type":"reflection","step":2,"plugin":"csv-packageplan-mapper","plugin_type":"skill","result_summary":"Skill loaded with full PackagePlan target structure, column mapping reference table, validations, defaults, and output format requirements.","insight":"I now have the complete mapping rules. I can directly apply the skill logic to the CSV data. The CSV has 13 rows. Key mappings: Id→packageId, Name→packageName, Desc→description, Price→price, Code→packageCode, CategoryName→packageCategory, Active→isActive, OnlineBooking→onlineBookingEnabled, PackageOwnerType→ownerType, PackageOwnerName→ownerName, Services (JSON)→benefits array. BusinessUnitId maps to businessId. The `Type` and `VersionId` columns have no target field match — they'll be skipped. TaxGroupName and CenterTaxId map to taxGroup/centerTaxId but skill says always null. BusinessUnit maps to businessName.","status":"success"}

{"type":"plan","query_summary":"User uploaded Packages_With_Services.csv with 13 package plan records — parse, map to PackagePlan JSON, and auto-save all 13 to Cosmos DB.","steps":[{"step":1,"name":"Load Skills","description":"Load execution-trace and csv-packageplan-mapper skills","status":"done"},{"step":2,"name":"Parse & Map CSV","description":"Apply mapper skill rules to all 13 CSV rows, build fully-formed PackagePlan JSON objects","status":"in-progress"},{"step":3,"name":"Save to Cosmos DB","description":"Call cosmos_create_document for all 13 records automatically","status":"todo"},{"step":4,"name":"Report Results","description":"Summarize records processed, saved, mapping used, and any issues","status":"todo"}]}

{"type":"thought","step":2,"plugin":"csv-packageplan-mapper","plugin_type":"skill","reasoning":"I have all the mapping rules from the skill. I'll now apply them to all 13 CSV rows. Key notes: Id→packageId (new GUID for 'id'), BusinessUnitId→businessId, BusinessUnit→businessName, CategoryName→packageCategory, Name→packageName, Desc→description, Code→packageCode, Price→price, Active→isActive, OnlineBooking→onlineBookingEnabled, PackageOwnerType→ownerType, PackageOwnerName→ownerName, Services JSON string→benefits array. taxGroup and centerTaxId always null. VersionId and Type have no target — skip. TaxGroupName has no target field (taxGroup always null per skill). Per the agent rules I must save ALL records automatically without asking the user. I'll fire all 13 cosmos_create_document calls simultaneously since they are independent of each other. partitionKey = 'PackagePlan', model = 'PackagePlan'.","expected_outcome":"13 Cosmos DB documents created successfully, each with a new system-assigned id.","status":"success"}</parameter>

{"type":"reflection","step":3,"plugin":"cosmos_create_document","plugin_type":"mcp","result_summary":"All 13 cosmos_create_document calls returned statusCode 201 — Record created successfully. Every record received a system-assigned GUID id and gocameomodel='PackagePlan' from Cosmos DB.","insight":"Perfect 13/13 success rate. All records are now persisted in Cosmos DB. I can now compile the final report.","status":"success"}
